response.justification: Bogdan brings a genuinely broad engineering background with strong TypeScript experience, hands-on work in high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests per second), and current involvement in AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq — directly relevant to the AI-native context of this role. However, Python is listed as a must-have and is entirely absent from his resume, skills section, and project history, which is a material gap for a Staff-level hire. His Staff title experience is recent and limited (one year at current role), with prior positions mostly at Senior level. The distributed systems depth, while present, is not consistently demonstrated across the breadth expected at Staff level.
strengths: Direct hands-on experience building and contributing to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks in current role — genuine AI-native exposure rather than adjacent
Proven high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second with low-latency, async I/O, and caching techniques
Strong TypeScript/Node.js fluency across multiple production environments, complemented by polyglot range (DLang, Java, C++) demonstrating systems-level thinking
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume, skills list, and all project history — this is an explicit must-have and represents a significant gap for a Staff-level candidate

Staff-level IC with technical strategy responsibilities is a recent designation; prior roles were predominantly Senior, and there is limited evidence of driving domain-wide technical decisions or leading cross-functional architectural initiatives
Operational ownership and incident-response experience (monitoring, on-call) are not mentioned anywhere in the resume, which is a stated must-have for this role
